dialogos.texto_en: Amazing.... It's so luxurious and splendid,\nyet has an elegance to it...\nYou'd guess this is where the king resides.
Are all the buildings around here the Seiden?
No, the Seiden refers to the building in front.\nThat one over there is the Nanden,\nand the one beyond that is the Hokuden.
Does this garden have a name too?
Yes, this garden is called the 'Una,'\nand apparently it's been a place where\nvarious ceremonies have been held since ancient times.
...Oh?\nIs there a tea room somewhere?
Hm? What brings that up?
Ah, it's because the guideboard over there\nsays that there's a tea service available.
Tea?
Uwaa, I can't read that.\nCan you help me out, Futaro-kun?
Yup. Sure thing. \nThis is pronounced 'teicha.'
I don't know that kind of tea.
No, it's not a type of tea.\nIt means serving tea to guests.
Ah, okay.\nI get it.
It seems like we can enjoy sanpin-cha\nand traditional Okinawan sweets.\nWould you like to participate?
As expected of you, Itsuki-chan.\nYou never miss a chance for food, do you?
N-no, that's not it!\nIt just happened to catch my eye!
Where are they holding it?\nIt can't be in the Seiden, right?
It seems like it takes place\nin a place called the Sasunoma,\nwhich is located further back.
Sasunoma?
Uuum,\nIt's like a waiting room for the prince,\nand it's a Japanese-style room with tatami mats.
A Japanese-style room in Shurijo Castle...\nthat sounds interesting.
It's also a perfect place to take a break.\nLet's go.
Yes!\nI'm looking forward to the tea and sweets!
(Haha, Itsuki's easily pleased.)
There are so many sweets!\nThey all look delicious.
So these are all traditional Okinawan sweets?\nThere are a lot of things\nI'm seeing for the first time.
I know this one. It's chinsuko, right?
What about this one?\nIt looks like a manju, but I wonder what it is.
It's written on the menu.\nThe one that looks like a manju is called 'kunpen,'\nand the one Itsuki has is called 'hanabouru.'
Hanabouru...\nThe name doesn't give me any idea of the taste.
Well then, let's try them and find out!
Mmm! So crispy!\nIt has a nice texture,\nand just the right amount of chewiness.
Hmm, it's more of a simple taste.
This 'chiirunkou' is a lot like castella.
The name is so cute!\nChiirunkou, chiirunkou.
Hehe, I know what you mean.\nIt's fun to say out loud.
Sanpin-cha is like jasmine tea, right?\nIt goes well with the sweets.
To be precise, the way it's brewed is different.
*sigh*\nAll of these sweets are so delicious.\nIt makes me happy.
(Itsuki really seems to enjoy eating everything,\n doesn't she.)
Oh, Uesugi-kun, you haven't touched anything yet.\nIf you don't want it, I'll take it...
I'm not giving it to you!
What a wonderful time!\nThe tea and sweets were delicious.
Yeah, there were so many sweets\nthat I ate for the first time.\nI'm glad we went.
I especially liked the hanabouru.\nAs the name hinted at,\nits delicate design reminded me of flowers.
Aren't you all about substance over style?\nI'm surprised you enjoyed the look over the taste.
A-aren't you rude!\nIt's important to enjoy how things look.
That's right.\nYou don't want to be told that by Fu-kun,\nwho only cares about stuffing his face.
Eerh...
...Anyway.
Wh-what is it?
Not only did it look good,\nit went really well with the tea.
Yes, the moderate sweetness\nmade the tea go down smoothly. I wanted seconds.
Haha, is it even accurate to say that tea\ngoes down smoothly? But it's true that you didn't just eat\nthe hanabouru, but devoured all the other sweets too.
Though it's too bad there weren't any leftovers.
I was only kidding before!
It doesn't sound like a joke when you say it,\nItsuki.
Yotsuba, you too? Gosh!
...Anyway.
Wh-what is it?
That delicate design was probably handmade.\nIt's the work of artisans.
Yes, it was almost too good to eat.
Yeah, but you had no hesitation in gulping it down.
I-I didn't want to waste any of it!
Besides, they're meant to be eaten. \nNot put on display, right?
Also, there are things you can only tell when you actually eat them.
Huh? Like what?
Like that the taste is pure, without any unnecessary\nelements. I felt their commitment to tradition\nin both taste and appearance.
...Anyway.
Wh-what is it?
You called me 'Uesugi-kun' earlier.
AH!
What happened to your enthusiasm from earlier?
Ooohh...
As I suspected, you're more interested in taste\nthan appearance. Once food is in front of you,\neverything else flies out of your head.
That's not true!\nI was just waiting for the right moment.\nI didn't forget!
Really, now.
REALLY!!!
You two over there.\nYou're being awfully loud again.
Ah...
Ooh...\nN-no, this is just...
Even though you say you don't want to show off \nbeing a couple, you're pretty oblivious to the fact \nthat you're flirting in public.
Sorry...
It's fine, it's fine.\nIt's only natural to gloat\nabout something like that.
But, not in front of so many people.
Okay...\nI'll be more mindful of my actions...
Now that you're more self-aware,\nshall we move on to the next place?\nThere's still a lot to see at Shurijo Castle!
Yes.
This still doesn't feel right...\nI need to be more...
Itsuki? You coming?
Ah, I'm coming now!
